title: Gas report - Week 24
summary:
LNG
The rates have crept up this week with activity reported in both basins. In the east, with some enquires reported, rates have risen by $3,612 since last Friday to close at $53,076 for BLNG1 (Gladstone/Japan). This is despite a recent report from the Japanese Ministry of Finance that the overall Japanese LNG imports have fallen by about 19.9% year-on-year in May.

children.title: LPG
children.summary: There was a slight hangover from the Nor-Shipping week, with many participants nursing heavy heads after a lovely sunny time in Oslo. Rates didn't react much but as the week wore on, an increasing tightness in ships East of Suez put cargoes working under pressure, some brokers recorded $110+ being bid, although at time of writing the index was yet to catch up while those who were working kept their cards close to their chest.
